summary: Eli Lilly is the dominant GLP-1 franchise (Mounjaro+Zepbound) with the largest pipeline in pharma history (42 active Phase III programs as of Q1 FY26) and a category-defining oral GLP-1 (orforglipron) submitted in 40+ countries and expected to launch in Q2 2026. TTM revenue $72.25B is growing +47.4% YoY with operating margins recently above 49%. Management raised FY26 revenue guidance by $2B at the Q1 call to $82-85B, implying ~26-31% growth on top of FY25's +44.7% growth. The valuation is rich (P/E FY1 26.1x vs peer median ~19x) but defensible given the growth differential. Manufacturing build-out exceeds $55B since 2020, the largest in company history. Stock pulled back 16% from $1,134 52w high to $948, creating a more reasonable entry into a compounder.
quotes: Since 2020, we've committed over $55 billion, the largest manufacturing build-out in company history.
We expect revenue to be between $80 billion and $83 billion. 2026 revenue guidance is $15 to $20 billion higher than that delivered in 2025.
We have increased the top and the bottom end of the revenue range by $2 billion and now expect full year revenue to be between $82 [billion] and $85 billion.
We submitted Orforglipron for obesity in the US and in more than 40 countries globally.
Finally, in cardiometabolic, Mounjaro and Suven global revenue was $12.8 billion combined, contributing $6.7 billion of growth compared to Q1 2025.
Q4 revenue growth -- 43% year over year, led by volume expansion in Mounjaro and Zepbound.
bull: Mounjaro+Zepbound exited Q4 FY25 with ~55% share of new US Type 2 diabetes incretin prescriptions and ~70% share of new branded obesity prescriptions. Both indications still in early innings of penetration globally.

First scalable oral GLP-1 small molecule submitted in 40+ countries. ATTAIN-1 showed 12.4% weight loss vs placebo; ATTAIN-MAINTAIN proved switching from injectable semaglutide maintained loss with only 0.9 kg average difference.
$55B+ committed since 2020 — largest pharma manufacturing build-out in history. New sites in Wisconsin and North Carolina operational; 1.8x more incretin doses produced in 2025 vs 2024 target met.

42 active Phase III programs as of Q1 FY26; 14 new Phase III programs initiated in recent months; 39 BD deals completed in 2025. Behind incretins: oloralintide (selective amylin), bimrenepatide, retatrutide (triple agonist).
Op margin expanded from 16.4% (FY24 Q1) to 49.4% (FY26 Q1), a 33pp expansion. Net margin moved 25.6% → 37.4% in same window. Gross margin steady at 80%+.
Q1 FY26 raise of $2B at both ends to $82-85B (vs prior $80-83B) signals sustained confidence in volume ramp and absorbing tariff/pricing headwinds.
bear: P/E FY1 26.1x represents +35% premium vs peer median (~19x). EV/EBITDA 25.7x is +86% premium vs peer median 13.8x. Multiple compression risk if growth decelerates.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) launching oral semaglutide; CVS/Caremark excluded Zepbound from template formularies (Jul 2025); generic semaglutide in some international markets pressuring international Mounjaro pricing.
US net pricing declined 7-8% in recent quarters. New Medicare access for obesity meds effective by Jul 2026 — Lilly's $50 out-of-pocket Part D access creates new headwinds while expanding TAM.
Quarterly CapEx running $2-2.5B/quarter; FCF often well below OCF; Q4 FY25 FCF only $0.68B vs OCF $3.22B. Manufacturing investment is necessary but suppresses near-term free cash flow.
Stock down 16% from $1,134 52w high; 1-year RSI shows price below SMA100 ($998) and bouncing off SMA200 ($916). ADX 18.7 = weak trend; momentum unclear.

Tirzepatide composition-of-matter patents in US expire ~2036, but secondary patents (formulation, methods of use) could be challenged earlier. Generic competition expected in some OUS markets even earlier given different IP regimes.
trigger_up: Orforglipron FDA approval Q2 2026 and launch execution
FY26 revenue raised again at Q2 FY26 call (Aug 5, 2026) above $85B high end
Retatrutide (triple agonist) Phase III data — successor to Zepbound with superior efficacy
Recovery to $1,000+ with break above SMA100 confirms uptrend resumption
trigger_down: Orforglipron FDA delay or efficacy concerns at launch
FY26 revenue guide cut or held flat at next call
Novo Nordisk gains share with oral semaglutide international launch
Tariff/pricing impact exceeds Lilly's current factored guidance assumptions
Break below $900 SMA200 — would invalidate uptrend and signal multiple compression
bottom_line: LLY is the clear category leader in obesity/diabetes (Mounjaro/Zepbound 70% obesity / 55% T2D share) with the strongest pipeline in pharma (42 Phase III programs, orforglipron oral GLP-1 launching Q2 2026). Management's 71.4% FY guidance hit-rate over 7 claims is above the 50% random baseline. The 16% drawdown from $1,134 to $948 creates entry into a structural compounder, though P/E FY1 of 26x prices in continued execution. We rate the setup as BULLISH with key catalysts: orforglipron approval/launch, FY26 guide revision at Q2 call (Aug 5, 2026), and operating margin sustainability above 50%.
disclaimer: Quoted statements verbatim from LLY earnings calls in /LLY/Transcripts/ directory. Fundamentals computed from EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0000059478). FactSet peer data captured 2026-05-11. Not investment advice.
